LAW AND MOTION 302, 3- CROSS-DEFENDANT XINYI TAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT. CROSS-DEFENDANT XINYI "VENTALI" TAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANT ZHEFEI "HERBIE" FU'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES FROM THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED. TAN'S MOTION IS PREMATURE. THERE IS NO REQUIREMENT A PARTY SEEKING ATTORNEY FEES UNDER CCP 1021.5 MUST PLEAD THEIR REQUEST IN AN ACTION TO ENFORCE IMPORTANT PUBLIC RIGHTS. (SNATCHKO V. WESTFIELD LLC (2010) 187 CAL.APP.4TH 469, 497.) HERE, ALTHOUGH A CITATION TO CCP 1021.5 IS MISSING FROM FU'S CROSS-COMPLAINT, THE OPPOSITION MAINTAINS FU'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS BASED ON THIS STATUTE. (OPP. 4:19-20.) ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT DENIES TAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. , CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

3- CROSS-DEFENDANT XINYI TAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant Xinyi "Ventali" Tan's motion to strike defendant Zhefei "Herbie" Fu's request for attorney fees from the cross-complaint is denied.

Tan's motion is premature. There is no requirement a party seeking attorney fees under CCP 1021.5 must plead their request in an action to enforce important public rights. (Snatchko v. Westfield LLC (2010) 187 Cal.App.4th 469, 497.) Here, although a citation to CCP 1021.5 is missing from Fu's cross-complaint, the opposition maintains Fu's request for attorney fees is based on this statute. (Opp. 4:19-20.) Accordingly, the court denies Tan's motion to strike.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. , Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Defendant ZHEFEI FU's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted. Defendant's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is brought under CCP 426.50. There is a policy of liberal construction of section 426.50 in order to avoid the forfeiture of causes of action. (Silver Orgs. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98.) "A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Id. at p. 98-99.) Bad faith is "not simply bad judgment or negligence, but rather the conscious doing of a wrong because of dishonest purpose or moral obliquity; it contemplates a state of mind affirmatively operating with furtive design or ill will." (Id. at p. 100.) Here, Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate bad faith.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)
